ai-w2-e1.answer: Ask AI to explain if-else with a real scenario (ticket pricing, grades). Then write your own version without looking.
ai-w2-e1.realWorld: Prompt: 'Explain if-else using movie ticket discount for seniors and students. Java only, with println.'

ai-w2-e2.answer: Ask AI to draw a loop as a table: iteration, i, output. Redraw the table by hand to verify.
ai-w2-e2.realWorld: Visual trace of a for-loop printing stars — catches off-by-one errors early.

ai-w2-e3.answer: Dry-run Fibonacci: track a, b each iteration on paper. Compare with AI's trace. Mismatch = learning moment.
ai-w2-e3.realWorld: Interviewers ask dry-runs — AI is practice partner, not answer sheet.
ai-w2-e3.code:
int a=0,b=1;
for(int i=0;i<5;i++){ System.out.print(a+" "); int n=a+b; a=b; b=n; }

ai-w2-e4.answer: Ask AI to explain recursion as 'function calling itself until base case' — no code yet. Draw the call stack on paper.
ai-w2-e4.realWorld: Preview before Week 8+ recursion deep-dive: factorial as 5! = 5 × 4!.

ai-w2-e5.answer: Paste nested loop finding duplicates. Ask for O(n) approach. Implement both; compare on array size 1000 vs 100000.
ai-w2-e5.realWorld: Measure, don't guess — AI's Big-O advice is theory until you time it.

ai-w2-e6.answer: Find bugs yourself first (15 min). Then ask AI. Compare solutions — did you miss the same bug?
ai-w2-e6.realWorld: Logic bug: loop uses <= length causing ArrayIndexOutOfBounds — classic AI and human catch.

ai-w2-e7.answer: Ask AI for 5 loop problems at your level. Solve without solution first. Use AI only for hints.
ai-w2-e7.realWorld: Generating practice beats passive reading — you need reps, not paragraphs.

ai-w2-e8.answer: Calculator edge cases: divide by zero, overflow, empty input, invalid operator. List them, then write tests.
ai-w2-e8.realWorld: AI lists cases; you own the test code — that's job-ready behavior.

ai-w2-e9.answer: Paste messy loop code. Ask: 'rename variables and add one comment per block.' Apply changes line by line.
ai-w2-e9.realWorld: Readability refactors are safe first AI-assisted edits — behavior stays same.

ai-w2-e10.answer: Paste slow code. Ask why it's slow (complexity, repeated work). Fix one issue and re-time.
ai-w2-e10.realWorld: Nested loop + repeated array scan — AI points to HashMap; you implement and verify.
